prompt: Using the provided reference image as the exact architectural reference — recreate the identical villa, identical camera position, identical composition, identical pool, identical palms, identical courtyard layout, in golden hour daylight instead of blue hour. The architecture must match the reference precisely: the same U-shaped plan with two flanking volumes and open central courtyard, the same angled upper roof geometry with angular overhangs, the same dark wood vertical slat cladding on the upper floor solid wall sections, the same white Calacatta marble structural columns and horizontal banding, the same long narrow lap pool running from the villa base toward the camera, the same two date palms one on each far side rooted in the white sand, the same open-air central courtyard with dining table and chairs, the same chandelier pendant above, the same media wall on the right side, the same LED strip lighting running along every overhang soffit edge. Change only the lighting and sky: the sun is 8 degrees above the western horizon directly behind the camera, casting near-horizontal warm golden-amber raking light across the entire facade. The white marble glows warm amber-ivory, right-facing surfaces noticeably brighter than left-facing surfaces which fall into soft warm shadow. The dark wood slat cladding becomes warm walnut-bronze in the raking sun. The LED strips along the soffits remain on but are now subtle against the ambient daylight — a soft warm amber hairline tracing every overhang edge. The deep courtyard is bathed in warm golden light with rich shadow play in the recessed areas and underside of the overhangs. The angled upper roof geometry casts dramatic diagonal shadow bands down the facade. The long narrow pool is copper-bronze, warm ripple highlights catching the low sun as fine bright horizontal lines across the surface. White sand beach on each side of the pool glows warm ivory-gold. The date palm trunks are lit golden, their fronds casting long dramatic shadow bands across the facade and roof. Sky: deep saturated amber-gold at the very top of the frame, burning into rich tangerine in the middle, vivid coral-red at the horizon line where it meets the Gulf — a dramatic Dubai golden hour sky, deeply saturated and cinematic, not pale, not washed out, not white. The Arabian Gulf visible on the horizon each side of the villa is deep copper-bronze. The composition is perfectly symmetrical and identical to the reference image in every structural and compositional aspect.
